questions.question: Why did Sue send for the doctor?
questions.answer: Because Johnsy was seriously ill with pneumonia.

questions.question: What did the doctor say about Johnsy’s chances of recovery?
questions.answer: He said she must have the will to live to recover.

questions.question: Why did Sue start painting in Johnsy’s room?
questions.answer: To distract Johnsy from her illness.

questions.question: Why was Johnsy counting the falling leaves?
questions.answer: She believed her life would end when the last leaf fell.

questions.question: What did Sue say when Johnsy talked about dying?
questions.answer: Sue told her that it was nonsense and tried to encourage her.

questions.question: Why did Sue call Behrman to her room?
questions.answer: To use him as a model for her painting.

questions.question: How did Behrman react when he heard Johnsy’s belief?
questions.answer: He became angry and called her foolish.

questions.question: What did Sue and Behrman see outside the window?
questions.answer: Only one ivy leaf remaining on the creeper.

questions.question: What was the weather like that night?
questions.answer: It was stormy with heavy rain and strong winds.

questions.question: What surprised Johnsy the next morning?
questions.answer: The last leaf had not fallen.

questions.question: How did Johnsy feel when she saw the leaf still there?
questions.answer: She began to regain hope.

questions.question: What realization did Johnsy have later?
questions.answer: She realized it was wrong to want to die.

questions.question: What did Sue give Johnsy after she felt better?
questions.answer: Hot soup and a mirror.

questions.question: What did Johnsy do after getting better?
questions.answer: She combed her hair and smiled.

questions.question: What did the doctor say about Johnsy’s recovery?
questions.answer: He said she would soon recover.

questions.question: Who else was suffering from pneumonia?
questions.answer: Behrman.

questions.question: Where was Behrman found by the janitor?
questions.answer: On his bed in a weak condition.

questions.question: What showed that Behrman had been outside in the storm?
questions.answer: His clothes and shoes were wet.

questions.question: What was lying near Behrman’s bed?
questions.answer: A ladder and a lantern.

questions.question: What paints were found near the ladder?
questions.answer: Green and yellow paints.

questions.question: What did Sue finally reveal to Johnsy?
questions.answer: That the last leaf was painted by Behrman.

questions.question: Why did Behrman paint the leaf?
questions.answer: To give Johnsy hope to live.

questions.question: What theme does the story highlight?
questions.answer: Hope and sacrifice.

questions.question: How did Sue support Johnsy throughout the story?
questions.answer: By caring for her and encouraging her.

questions.question: Why is Behrman remembered in the story?
questions.answer: For his selfless sacrifice.
